nodes.label: electron-builder Packaging Tool
nodes.rationale: Selected over electron-forge because it is a drop-in that reads the existing dist/ output with no project restructure required, avoids conflicts with Nx targets, and is the dominant pattern in Nx+Electron monorepos. Configuration lives in a single electron-builder.yml alongside project.json.

nodes.label: electron-forge (Rejected Alternative)
nodes.rationale: Considered but rejected because it requires migrating to Forge's own build pipeline, which conflicts with existing Nx targets. Less common with Nx monorepos.

nodes.label: Asar Bundle and Runtime File Strategy
nodes.rationale: Files inside the asar are read-only. Write-able resources (SQLite DB via Prisma, migration SQL files, schema.prisma) must live outside the asar in resources/ or userData. The SQLite .db file is created fresh in userData on first launch via prisma migrate deploy and must never be placed inside the asar. Prisma query engine binary is a native binary and must not be inside asar either.
nodes.norm_label: asar bundle and runtime file strategy

nodes.label: SmartNgRX Signal-Based Reactive Pattern
nodes.rationale: SmartNgRX uses progressive data loading where skeleton rows arrive first, then get filled in with actual data via Angular signals. BaseTableComponent was refactored from imperative initDataSource()/RxJS Observable pattern to a declarative signal-based [data] input so the table automatically reacts when source signals change, eliminating manual refresh() calls and making it compatible with SmartNgRX.
